---
title: "Claim Ledgers and Belief Revision"
chapter_id: "claim-ledgers-and-belief-revision"
part_id: "planning-memory-reasoning-execution"
status: "conceptual"
last_updated: "2026-06-24"
primary_sources:
  - "spinoza"
  - "viea"
  - "coherence_exchange"
  - "aletheia"
  - "uat"
evidence_level: "argument"
open_evidence_gaps:
  - "Assigned source texts have not yet been ingested or summarized."
  - "No Codex tests have been implemented or run."
  - "Chapter prose is a scaffold, not a completed manuscript draft."
---

# Claim Ledgers and Belief Revision

## Chapter status

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Chapter ID | `claim-ledgers-and-belief-revision` |
| Part | Part II - Planning, Memory, Reasoning, and Execution |
| Status | conceptual |
| Last updated | 2026-06-24 |
| Primary source records | `spinoza`, `viea`, `coherence_exchange`, `aletheia`, `uat` |
| Evidence level | argument |
| Source ingestion state | Source records assigned; source texts not yet ingested. |
| Test state | Planned only; no tests have been run. |

## Drafting guardrail

This stub is derived from `book_structure.json` and the source inventory only. It does not claim that the listed source documents have been ingested, summarized, or independently verified.

## Problem

The architecture needs a durable epistemic layer for claims, evidence, contradictions, uncertainty, and revision.

## Why existing approaches are insufficient

A model can generate plausible prose without maintaining why it believes a claim or how new evidence changes prior beliefs.

## Core claim

[claim-ledgers-and-belief-revision.core, support: argument] Reasoning should maintain claim ledgers with support states, provenance, contradiction links, uncertainty, and revision history.

## Mechanism

- Extract claims into structured records.
- Attach evidence tier, provenance, and uncertainty.
- Revise beliefs when contradictions or stronger evidence appear.

## Interfaces

- VCM supplies source context.
- Spinoza verifies claims.
- Evidence matrix records book-level claims.

## Invariants

- Claims are separable from prose.
- Contradictions remain linked.
- Revision history is not overwritten.

## Failure modes

- Untracked assumptions.
- Belief inertia.
- Contradiction deletion.

## Minimal implementation

A claim-record schema and belief-revision transition example.

## Codex test plan

| Test | Purpose | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Claim extraction test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Contradiction detection test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Belief revision test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |

## Source crosswalk

| Source ID | Title | Planned use |
|---|---|---|
| `spinoza` | Proof of Belief / The Spinoza Architecture | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Neurosymbolic belief, transparent axiomatic AI belief systems, verification, belief revision. |
| `viea` | Verified Intent-to-Execution Architecture | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Keystone source. Human intent -> command contracts -> artifacts -> routing -> runtime targets -> verification -> deployment -> feedback. |
| `coherence_exchange` | The Coherence Exchange | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Found in AI generated paper dump. Use carefully; speculative synthesis of PlanForge, Spinoza, Talos, UAT, Alignment Field. |
| `aletheia` | Aletheia Foundry | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Safe general intelligence predecessor. Use ideas; avoid final branding if collision remains. |
| `uat` | Unified Adaptive Tribunal | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Multi-AI collaborative refinement/evaluation protocol. |

## Summary

This chapter currently establishes the structural slot for this layer of the ASI stack. The next drafting pass should ingest assigned sources, separate source-derived claims from design hypotheses, and update Appendix C when support states change.
